5.Number & Operations in Base Ten: Numbers in base ten are composed of place values. Each digit represents a value based on its position (ones, tens, hundreds, etc.). Understanding a number means recognizing how many groups of tens and ones it contains.
5.Number & Operations—Fractions: A fraction represents equal parts of a whole. The denominator shows how many equal parts the whole is divided into, and the numerator shows how many of those parts are being considered.
5.Operations & Algebraic Thinking: Multiplication can be understood as repeated addition. It represents the total number of items in equal-sized groups.
